authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(104) — Sisquoc River and tributaries (Santa Barbara Co.)
sources.quotedText: (104) Sisquoc River and tributaries (Santa Barbara Co.). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 249525, "Sisquoc River", Stream, CA, Santa Barbara Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":249525,"gaz_name":"Sisquoc River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA"} — geometry multipoint; first point converts to lon -120.3124, lat 34.9030, near the river's mouth at its confluence with the Cuyama River (forming the Santa Maria River), correcting the file's prior coordinate (-120.0500, 34.8400) which sat ~25km upstream along the mainstem and did not match any GNIS-derived point.

parameters.note: The Sisquoc River (a major tributary of the Santa Maria River, largely within Los Padres National Forest) and all its tributaries, including Manzana and Tepusquet creeks, are closed to protect federally endangered Southern California steelhead, which historically supported the second-largest steelhead run in Santa Barbara County.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, including all tributaries
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. (This water is closed to fishing all year; the license requirement is noted for statewide consistency only.)

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
